content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Melvin Shapiro 
3786 Alabama Street, B-306 
San Diego, CA 
92104 
Dear Mr. Shapiro: 
June 3, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. l-87-121 
On April 17, 1987, we received your letter dated April 15, 
1987. 
Unfortunately, it was misplaced. 
This is not a common 
occurrence in this agency, but it can happen. 
I hope you will 
accept my sincere apology for the delay in responding. 
You have written with concerns about the San Diego 
Community College District Foundation. 
You believe that its 
directors should be filing statements of economic interest 
(SEls), but they apparently have not done so. 
The Political Reform Act !I requires the Commission to 
provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under 
the Act are in question. 
The Commission does not provide 
advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another 
person's duties under the Act. 
(Regulation 18329(b) (8) (B); 
copy enclosed) 
Consequently, we are not able to provide advice 
on the issue you raise in your letter. 
Let me note, generally, that directors of community college 
auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEls unless 
they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of 
interest code. 
You have indicated that the San Diego 
foundation has not so designated its directors. 
You might 
raise this issue with the foundation if you have not done so 
already. 
DMG: jaj 
;(,/-'. 
/(. 
Diane M. Griffitns<-
General Counsel 
!I Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


MELVIN SHAPIRO 
3786 Alabama St. B-306 
San Diego, CA 92104 
ern: 
e 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Melvyn Shapiro 
3786 Alabama Street, B-306 
San Diego, Ca 
92104 
Dear Mr. Shapiro: 
April 21, 1987 
Re: 
87-121 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on April 17, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If your request is for informal assistance, we will 
answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request tor 
disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
;J /r 
(( 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another person's duties under the Act. Consequently, we are not able to provide advice on the issue you raise in your letter. Generally, directors of community college auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEIs unless they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.
sections.question_synthetic: Should directors of the San Diego Community College District Foundation be filing statements of economic interest (SEIs)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice to third parties about another person's filing obligations, but notes that community college foundation directors are generally not required to file SEIs unless designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two related letters: acknowledgment letter (April 21, 1987) and substantive response letter (June 3, 1987). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections; extracted from letter body. FPPC declines advice based on Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should directors of the San Diego Community College District Foundation be filing statements of economic interest (SEIs)?

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another person's duties under the Act. Consequently, we are not able to provide advice on the issue you raise in your letter. Generally, directors of community college auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEIs unless they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.

embedding.summary: The FPPC received an inquiry from a third party (Melvin Shapiro) about whether San Diego Community College District Foundation directors should file statements of economic interest. The FPPC declined to provide formal advice on third-party inquiries but provided general information that such directors are typically not required to file SEIs unless specifically designated.
